
## Muscle [[Flexibility]] tests

|                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Equipment used | - Goniometer<br><br>- Manual<br>- Electrical<br>- Halo<br><br>- Inclinometer<br>- CROM BROM<br>- Measuring tape                                                                                                                |
| Technique      | Can be active or passive:<br><br>1. Place patient in position where muscle group is isolated<br>2. Perform movement while spotting for compensation, tension, discomfort etc<br>3. Measure ROM or muscle length with equipment |
|                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

## Muscle strength tests
### Manual Muscle Test

Follows Kendall Scale
Scale from grades 0-5

|   |   |
|---|---|
|Grade|Description|
|0|Zero, no activity|
|1|Trace activity (slight contraction) +palpable twitches|
|2|Poor, cannot work against gravity, full ROM when gravity minimised|
|3|Fair Overcome gravity|
|4|Good, cannot overcome resistance|
|5|Normal, can overcome resistance|

Techniques:
1. Active Resistance:(unreliable)
	Apply resistance gradually until patient max tolerable level and movement ceases
2. Break Test; only for >G4
	Apply resistance at end range and ask patient to maintain
	If 'break' = G4, no 'break' = G5
3. One-rep max test
	Never recommended for regular patients
	Amount of load patient can move one time  thru full ROM and good control +posture

### dynamometry

1. Isokinetic:
	- Machine that measures peak torque and force generation
	- Reliable, valid responsive
![Bengaluru FC on X: "No, we're not sending the skipper on a mission to  space. Instead, we put him to the test on the Biodex Isokinetic muscle  dynamometer. #WeAreBFC #RoomForMore https://t.co/ENRZP6yhHv" /](data:image/jpeg;base64,)
 
2. Hand Held
	- Similar to MMT but resistance applied is more consistent
	- Used along 'break' test
	- Reliable + valid, responsiveness requires further research
![[Pasted image 20250126211118.png]]
### Elastic bend
Muscle strength is defined by the elastic band with the lowest elasticity that the patient can overcome.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### Cable tensiometry

Measures tension overcome by muscle groups

![Baseline Cable Tensiometer | center | 250](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

### Body weight
Use of body weight as loading metric e.g. IPPT
